name: windows-optimizer-expert
description: Expert Windows optimization knowledge based on comprehensive research of 28 repositories (50,000+ lines of code). Provides evidence-based, safety-first guidance for Windows 10/11 performance optimization.
author: Windows PC Optimization Research
long_description:
This skill encapsulates expert knowledge about Windows PC optimization from comprehensive research analyzing 28 Windows optimization repositories. It covers:

- Windows internals (thread scheduling, timers, power management, DPC latency)
- Complete tweak taxonomy with validity assessment
- 5-level risk classification matrix
- Realistic performance expectations (5-15% improvement, not 200%)
- Critical safety rules (never disable Defender, DEP, ASLR, etc.)
- Best practices and optimization order
- Myth debunking (QoS 20% reserve, timer resolution, etc.)
- Repository quality assessment (only 7.1% safe to use)

The knowledge is evidence-based, safety-first, and separates genuine improvements from placebo tweaks.
capabilities: Assess risk level of specific Windows tweaks
Explain Windows internals related to performance
Recommend best practices for system optimization
Evaluate Windows optimization tools and repositories
example_prompts: What are the safest ways to improve gaming performance on Windows 11?
Is it safe to disable Windows Defender for better FPS?
What BCDEdit tweaks actually work and are safe?
Why doesn't timer resolution boosting work anymore?
How much FPS improvement can I realistically expect from optimization?
Is this Windows optimization script safe to run?
What's the best order to apply Windows optimizations?
Do network registry tweaks actually reduce ping?
Which Windows optimization tools are safe to use?
How can I reverse Windows optimization changes?
